description: Claude Code usage — cost, tokens, cache efficiency, and development output
title: Claude Code

panels.title: Real-Time Cost Burn Rate
panels.description: Current spend rate annualized from the past 30 minutes. Won't zero out between API turns since the window is wider than a typical turn gap. Green below $0.50/hr, yellow to $2/hr, red above.

panels.title: Total Cost Today
panels.description: Total API spend in the last 24 hours alongside the 7-day rolling daily average. Compare the two to see if today is running above or below baseline.

panels.title: Subagent Cost (24h)
panels.description: Cost from subagent tasks — parallel research, code review, and background work — in the last 24 hours. High subagent cost relative to main session cost means Claude is spawning many parallel workers.

panels.title: Main Session Cost (24h)
panels.description: Cost from main conversation turns in the last 24 hours. This is the direct cost of your prompts and Claude's responses, excluding subagent and auxiliary tasks.

panels.title: Code Edit Acceptance Rate %
panels.description: Percentage of Claude's proposed file edits that were accepted in the last 24 hours, plus the 7-day average. Below 80% suggests edits are frequently being rejected or need rework.

panels.title: Cost Forecast
panels.description: Daily and monthly cost projections extrapolated from the current 6-hour burn rate. Useful for catching runaway sessions before the bill arrives.

panels.title: Average Cost / Session
panels.description: Average API spend per session in the last 24 hours, compared to the 7-day average. A rising number over time usually means sessions are running longer without being compacted.

panels.title: Cost per 1K Tokens
panels.description: Effective cost per 1,000 tokens across all token types. Because cache reads are ~10% of input price, heavy cache usage pulls this number down significantly. Compare against the 7-day average to spot efficiency changes.

panels.title: Active Time (24h)
panels.description: CLI time is how long Claude Code was running and processing. User time is how long you were actively engaged. A high CLI-to-user ratio means Claude is doing a lot of autonomous work.

panels.title: Token Distribution by Model (24h)
panels.description: Share of total tokens consumed by each model in the last 24 hours. Sonnet dominating is expected for most workloads. A large Opus slice warrants a check on whether those tasks needed Opus-level reasoning.

panels.title: Active Sessions (24h)
panels.description: Number of Claude Code sessions in the last 24 hours compared to the 7-day daily average.

panels.title: Average Session Metrics
panels.description: Per-session averages over the last 24 hours: cost in dollars, total tokens, and active CLI time. Rising cost or token averages over days indicate sessions are running longer without being compacted.

panels.title: Lines of Code Modified
panels.description: Lines added and removed in the last 24 hours alongside rolling 7-day daily averages. A large gap between Today and 7 Day Avg indicates an unusually active or quiet day.

panels.title: Cache Hit Rate %
panels.description: Percentage of input-side tokens served from Anthropic's prompt cache. Above 80% is healthy. Below 60% means cache is not being reused effectively — sessions may be too short or context structure is breaking cache blocks.

panels.title: Total Tokens Today
panels.description: All tokens consumed in the last 24 hours across all types, alongside the 7-day daily average.

panels.title: Model Token Efficiency (tokens/$)
panels.description: Total tokens per dollar spent by model over the selected time range. Higher is more efficient. Haiku should significantly outperform Opus. If the gap is narrow, check whether model selection is being forced.

panels.title: Tool Usage Breakdown
panels.description: Share of tool calls by tool type over the selected time range, sourced from structured logs. Covers all tools — Bash, Read, Edit, Write, and others — not just file edits.

panels.title: Peak Cost Hours
panels.description: API spend per hour over the selected time range. Spikes show when sessions were most expensive. Useful for correlating high cost periods with what was being worked on.

panels.title: Weekly Total Token Usage
panels.description: Total tokens consumed in the last 7 days. The sparkline shows the daily trend — a rising slope means usage is accelerating week over week.

panels.title: Cost Anomaly Detection
panels.description: Deviation of current hourly spend from the 7-day historical average for the same hour. Values above 200% (dashed line) are flagged as anomalies — investigate what was running during those periods.

panels.title: Prompts Per Hour
panels.description: Count of user prompt events logged over a rolling 1-hour window, sourced from structured logs. Peaks show concentrated interaction periods; gaps are idle time between sessions.

panels.title: Tool Decision Sources (24h)
panels.description: How tool execution decisions were authorized over the last 24 hours. Config = approved via CLAUDE.md or settings. User temporary = approved once for the session. User permanent = added to always-allow list.

panels.title: Code Modification Velocity (lines/min)
panels.description: Rate of lines added and removed per minute. Spikes indicate concentrated editing bursts. A high removed rate relative to added suggests refactoring or cleanup sessions.

panels.title: Token Usage Rate (24h)
panels.description: Tokens consumed per minute across all types. The legend table shows mean, last, and peak rates for the selected window. A sustained high rate approaching your plan's TPM ceiling is the signal to start a fresh session.

panels.title: Prompt Length Distribution (chars)
panels.description: Distribution of prompt lengths by character count bucket over the selected time range, sourced from structured logs. Most prompts are short — long prompts (1k+ chars) typically mean pasted code or detailed task descriptions.

panels.title: Effective Tokens — All Models (24h)
panels.description: Actual effective (non-cacheRead) tokens consumed across all models in the last 24 hours, with a 7-day sparkline showing the daily trend. These are the tokens that count toward Anthropic's usage limits (cacheRead is excluded). This is a real measured volume, not a percentage of limit — OTel telemetry cannot reconstruct Anthropic's quota gauge because the weekly reset boundary is not exported. For your true remaining quota, run /usage in the Claude Code CLI.

panels.title: Effective Tokens — Sonnet Only (24h)
panels.description: Actual effective (non-cacheRead) Sonnet tokens consumed in the last 24 hours, with a 7-day sparkline trend. Sonnet has its own separate usage cap at Anthropic, which is why it's broken out here. This is a real measured volume, not a percentage of limit — the weekly reset boundary needed to compute a true percentage is not exported in OTel telemetry. Run /usage in the Claude Code CLI for your actual remaining quota.

panels.title: Top Skills by Cost (24h)
panels.description: API spend attributed to named skills in the last 24 hours. The skill_name label is set whenever Claude Code invokes a skill. Turns without a skill are excluded. High-cost skills often have large context windows or expensive prompt templates worth reviewing.

panels.title: Top Skills by Effective Tokens (24h)
panels.description: Effective (non-cacheRead) token consumption per skill in the last 24 hours. A skill with high tokens here but low cost in the panel to the left is getting strong cache reuse. A skill high in both is a genuine cost driver.

panels.title: MCP Server Token Attribution (24h)
panels.description: Token consumption attributed to MCP server calls in the last 24 hours. Only turns that invoked an MCP tool carry the mcp_server_name label. Shows which MCP integrations (e.g. linear) are driving context size and cost.

panels.title: Cost by Effort Level (24h)
panels.description: API spend grouped by the effort setting (low / medium / high / max) in the last 24 hours. Effort controls thinking-token budget. A large share of spend at high or max effort is worth checking against whether those tasks actually needed deep reasoning.

panels.title: Commits (24h)
panels.description: Git commits made via Claude Code in the last 24 hours versus the 7-day daily average. Pairs with the lines-of-code panels to show shipped output, not just edit volume.

panels.title: API Errors (24h)
panels.description: Count of api_error events in the last 24 hours — API failures including rate-limit rejections, network errors, and model errors. Green at zero; any errors turn the panel yellow, 5+ turns it red. Drill into the Logs dashboard for detail.

panels.title: API Response Latency p95 by Model
panels.description: 95th percentile API response duration by model over the selected range, from structured api_request logs. Higher Sonnet latency vs Haiku reflects longer reasoning chains. Sustained spikes above baseline signal context-window pressure or API backpressure. Yellow at 30s, red at 60s.

panels.title: Estimated Cache Savings (24h)
panels.description: Approximate dollars saved by prompt caching in the last 24 hours, versus the 7-day average. Computed as cacheRead tokens × (input price − cache read price): Sonnet $2.70/1M, Haiku $0.72/1M. An estimate — actual savings depend on exact model mix, but Sonnet dominates usage here.

panels.title: Effective Tokens by Source
panels.description: Hourly effective (non-cacheRead) tokens stacked by query_source: main (direct conversation turns), auxiliary (background context builds), and subagent (parallel spawned agents). A rising subagent share means Claude is doing more autonomous orchestration relative to interactive work.
